answer:
**There is no discussion of *bodhicitta* in *Kalpabuddha*, Chapter 3.**[^1]

This short chapter (2 paragraphs, 41 words) describes Lord Ratnasambhava's attributes in the context of a mandala or deity yoga framework: his direction (South), color (yellow), consort (Lady Māmakī), mantra (Trāṃ), jñāna (ākāśamata), implement (kapāla), and abode (inside of the spleen).[^1][^2]

*Bodhicitta* ("enlightenment mind") does appear in other works in the corpus, such as *San Hyan Kamahayanikan* (Ch. 1) and *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana* (Ch. 1), but not here or anywhere else in *Kalpabuddha*.[^3]

[^1]: *Kalpabuddha*, Ch. 3 (full chapter), [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kalpabuddha-d760dc/03/translation/).

[^2]: *Kalpabuddha*, Ch. 3, ¶¶ [p-f7acd5](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kalpabuddha-d760dc/03/translation/#p-f7acd5), [p-68d96e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kalpabuddha-d760dc/03/translation/#p-68d96e).

[^3]: Corpus search for "bodhicitta" (0 hits in *Kalpabuddha*).
